content: Step-wise Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for sequential agent reasoning can be structured to mimic the iterative cooperative interaction between the prefrontal cortex and hippocampus during memory retrieval, where each step of GRPO policy refinement corresponds to a back-and-forth neural signaling exchange that prioritizes relevant memory retrieval for multi-step reasoning. This neurobiological constraint naturally solves the working memory capacity limit that plagues current step-wise GRPO agents by only activating and reinforcing memory traces relevant to the current reasoning step, rather than retaining all accumulated context.
